title: A way to sort/alphabetize groups
excerpt: Instead of having to drag groups into desired place, there should be a way to sort

posts.author: mbetancur (Maria Betancur)
posts.cookedHtml: Instead of having to drag groups into desired place, there should be a way to sort

posts.author: sheilasimon (Sheila Simon)
posts.cookedHtml: We save several projects in one board and it would be great to be able to sort the groups automatically in alphabetical order.

posts.author: CPR (Chuck Rothberg)
posts.cookedHtml:
I know that this has been posted, but it’s a closed thread:

https://community.monday.com/t/ordering-groups-within-board-alphabetically/4244/7

It apparently has been submitted as a feature request. Is there any update on this because I know I could certainly make use of sorting groups alphabetically. I think it’s possible via the API, but that’s not practical for daily use.

posts.author: amccombs (Amanda McCombs)
posts.cookedHtml: I second this…as do many others. It’s just bonkers that the ability to sort a board alphabetically by project – the master categories, no less-- does not exist?!

posts.author: giangpham (Giang Pham)
posts.cookedHtml: Yeah, I’m waiting for the monday.com team to reply on this as well.

posts.author: goldmandw (Dylan)
posts.cookedHtml: Please, PLEASE make a way to sort groups. We have a board for installation and maintenance and would like to be able to sort by dates of install or service.

posts.author: CNunez (Christianne Nunez)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi. Our company is very new at Monday.com and we already have tons of items within same boards. Has there been an update on how to organize in alpha order?

posts.author: APICaptain (Calum Klesel)
posts.cookedHtml:
I see the value in sorting groups and I agree this should be a feature.

However, implement this design change to achieve your desired results today:

Instead of using a Group to separate out your projects, use a Status Column.
The order of the labels will the the order of the grouping when you then Group by the Status column. You can also “Group by” other columns; such as a text or date column.
Save the Grouping to a new View and you can sort to your heart’s content.
Then think of the Groups in different terms for your Main Table. I like to use Current/Active, Past, and Future Groups for my project based boards done this way.

image1560×461 58 KB

posts.author: DevdeVince (Vincent Coval - Devdevils)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @APICaptain and @mbetancur ,

we just released an app called Groups Manager.
It allows you to create/reorder/archive/delete and in your case, to sort groups alphabetically.
Check it out : monday.com: Apps Marketplace

I hope it will fit your needs.

Vince from Devdevils

posts.author: Joe_Downey (Joe Downey)
posts.cookedHtml: Has the “group by” been removed?

posts.author: badoorty (David )
posts.cookedHtml: أؤيد فرز اللوحة أبجدية حسب المشروع

posts.author: APICaptain (Calum Klesel)
posts.cookedHtml: No it hasn’t. If you’re not seeing a group-by option for the column you want to use, it’s probably due to the column type. The group-by option is incompatible with columns that allow an item to belong in multiple groups. So, you can’t use a linked board that allows selecting multiple items, or a multi-select dropdown, etc. Try it with a Status column to verify you can find the option.

posts.author: cat.goliath (Cat)
posts.cookedHtml: Ability to mass-delete groups, as well!!

posts.author: kacper (Kacper Marciniak)
posts.cookedHtml: We’ll get GTA 6 before we can alphabetically sort groups in Monday.com

posts.author: PayWay369 (paytonwest@hotmail.com)
posts.cookedHtml: this is getting to be insane how they cannot implement basic sorting that a 8th grade developer can resolve. my users are giving up on the application and i cant hold them back much longer

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml: With the release of the Group By feature, the ability to auto-sort groups becomes even more desirable for our organization. Per earlier comments you can group nicely on status columns. We can accomplish a lot with using Group By on Text columns also now though, and if we could then sort the groups, a very robust process for automated board setup materializes. This is especially applicable for ongoing monthly and weekly metrics boards and such.

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml:
Wow, well I was thrilled to come back from the holidays and find the ability to sort Groups in place in our account!

Thanks Monday!

Screenshot 2025-01-10 at 3.15.43 PM582×320 14.8 KB

posts.author: marco-berlin (marco)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Heather,

Thanks for sharing.

Sorry to have to ask again.

The requirement was to sort a large number of groups by name from A-Z in a board.

But that still doesn’t work? Instead, I can only sort by columns in the elements.

Please explain exactly how you sort by the names of the groups.
Thank you.

Marco

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @marco-berlin in our account I find that the Group By feature I screenshotted allows for sorting based upon different criteria dependent upon the column type. For example, if it is a Status column it provides the option to sort by the order of the labels or alphabetical. If it is a text column then it provides the option to sort alphabetically.
